args.code:
import pandas as pd
import json
import ast

# Load data
reviews_data = locals()['var_function-call-14279537980861774924']
if isinstance(reviews_data, str) and reviews_data.endswith('.json'):
    with open(reviews_data, 'r') as f:
        reviews_data = json.load(f)

business_data = locals()['var_function-call-13729130577212114888']
if isinstance(business_data, str) and business_data.endswith('.json'):
    with open(business_data, 'r') as f:
        business_data = json.load(f)

# Process reviews
# Get set of business IDs that had reviews in 2018
reviewed_business_ids = set()
for item in reviews_data:
    ref = item.get('business_ref')
    if ref and ref.startswith('businessref_'):
        bid = ref.replace('businessref_', '')
        reviewed_business_ids.add(bid)

# Process businesses
# Check for parking
parking_business_ids = set()

for bus in business_data:
    attrs = bus.get('attributes')
    
    # Check if attributes is a dict (it might be None or "None")
    if not isinstance(attrs, dict):
        continue
        
    has_bike = False
    has_car = False
    
    # Bike Parking
    # It seems to be a string "True" or "False"
    bike_val = attrs.get('BikeParking')
    if bike_val == 'True':
        has_bike = True
        
    # Business Parking
    car_val = attrs.get('BusinessParking')
    if car_val:
        # It's a string representation of a dict
        try:
            # We need to handle potential format issues if any
            # The sample shows "{'garage': False, ...}"
            parking_dict = ast.literal_eval(car_val)
            if isinstance(parking_dict, dict):
                # Check if any value is True
                if any(parking_dict.values()):
                    has_car = True
        except Exception as e:
            # parsing failed, assume False
            pass
            
    if has_bike or has_car:
        bid_raw = bus.get('business_id')
        if bid_raw and bid_raw.startswith('businessid_'):
            bid = bid_raw.replace('businessid_', '')
            parking_business_ids.add(bid)

# Intersection
result_ids = reviewed_business_ids.intersection(parking_business_ids)
count = len(result_ids)

print("__RESULT__:")
print(count)

var_function-call-15665480755176242907.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_function-call-15665480755176242907.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

args.code:
import pandas as pd
import json
import ast

# Load reviews data (from previous call)
reviews_data = locals()['var_function-call-14279537980861774924']
if isinstance(reviews_data, str) and reviews_data.endswith('.json'):
    with open(reviews_data, 'r') as f:
        reviews_data = json.load(f)

# Load business data (from new call)
business_data_path = locals()['var_function-call-365032499311372224']
with open(business_data_path, 'r') as f:
    business_data = json.load(f)

# Process reviews
reviewed_business_ids = set()
for item in reviews_data:
    ref = item.get('business_ref')
    if ref and ref.startswith('businessref_'):
        bid = ref.replace('businessref_', '')
        reviewed_business_ids.add(bid)

# Process businesses
parking_business_ids = set()

for bus in business_data:
    attrs = bus.get('attributes')
    
    # Check if attributes is a dict
    if not isinstance(attrs, dict):
        continue
        
    has_bike = False
    has_car = False
    
    # Bike Parking
    bike_val = attrs.get('BikeParking')
    if bike_val == 'True':
        has_bike = True
        
    # Business Parking
    car_val = attrs.get('BusinessParking')
    if car_val:
        try:
            parking_dict = ast.literal_eval(car_val)
            if isinstance(parking_dict, dict):
                # Check if any value is True
                if any(parking_dict.values()):
                    has_car = True
        except Exception as e:
            pass
            
    if has_bike or has_car:
        bid_raw = bus.get('business_id')
        if bid_raw and bid_raw.startswith('businessid_'):
            bid = bid_raw.replace('businessid_', '')
            parking_business_ids.add(bid)

# Intersection
result_ids = reviewed_business_ids.intersection(parking_business_ids)
count = len(result_ids)

print("__RESULT__:")
print(count)

var_function-call-15665480755176242907.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened
